title: Wire parsed ESP32 BLE values into dashboard cards and chart

requirements.summary: Update the BLE parser in bleNus.ts to parse the pipe-delimited format BAT:85|LAT:2050|VAL:450 into structured numeric fields
acceptanceCriteria: Parser splits on `|` before extracting each field.
Returns numeric types, not strings, for all three fields.

file_operations.description: Add or update the ESP32 pipe-delimited parser function to split the incoming BLE notification string by `|`, then extract the numeric values after `BAT:`, `LAT:`, and `VAL:` prefixes using string operations and parseInt/parseFloat. Return a typed object with { battery: number, latency: number, val: number }. Ensure the parser handles the exact format `BAT:85|LAT:2050|VAL:450` and returns numeric types for all three fields.

requirements.summary: Update BluetoothContext to use the pipe-delimited parser on BLE notifications and store battery, latency, and val fields in context state
acceptanceCriteria: On receiving a BLE notification with the format `BAT:85|LAT:2050|VAL:450`, the context updates its `battery`, `actuationLatency`, and `lightLevel` (or equivalent `val`) state fields.
No stale or hardcoded fallback values remain in the notification handler path.

file_operations.description: Update the BLE NUS characteristic notification handler to import and invoke the pipe-delimited parser from bleNus.ts when a notification arrives. Replace any existing ad-hoc parsing logic with the parser call. Store the returned `battery`, `latency`, and `val` fields into the context state (mapping `val` to `lightLevel` or an appropriate state field). Ensure that the state updates correctly reflect the parsed values and remove any hardcoded or stale fallback values in the notification handler path.

requirements.summary: Wire the battery value from BluetoothContext into BatteryIndicator card to display live percentage
acceptanceCriteria: Battery card shows the integer parsed from `BAT:` in the BLE string.
When the BLE value is 85, the card displays `85%`.
The progress bar and color-coded icon reflect the live value.

file_operations.description: Update the component to consume the `battery` value (0–100) from BluetoothContext using useContext or a custom hook. Display the numeric percentage in the card's UI, ensuring it updates live as new BLE notifications arrive. Verify that the progress bar and color-coded icon logic already in the component correctly reflect the live battery value.

requirements.summary: Wire the latency value from BluetoothContext into ActuationLatencyCard to display live milliseconds
acceptanceCriteria: Actuation Latency card shows the integer parsed from `LAT:` in the BLE string.
When the BLE value is 2050, the card displays `2050 ms`.
The displayed value updates live as new notifications arrive.

file_operations.description: Update the component to consume the `latency` value (milliseconds integer) from BluetoothContext using useContext or a custom hook. Display the live latency value in the card's UI with the `ms` suffix, ensuring it updates as new BLE notifications arrive.

requirements.summary: Feed the val field (light level) from BluetoothContext into the real-time light level chart
acceptanceCriteria: The real-time light level chart plots data points sourced from the `VAL:` field of each BLE notification.
New data points are appended with the current timestamp on each notification.
The chart updates visually as new BLE values arrive.

file_operations.description: Update the real-time light level chart logic to consume the `val` field (light level) from BluetoothContext. On each incoming BLE notification, append a new data point (timestamp + `val` value) to the chart's rolling data series. Ensure the chart's rendering logic updates visually to reflect the new data points as they arrive.
